touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Isaac Sharma introducing our AI meeting assistant for CS teams. Highlighted productivity gains and automation capabilities compared to manual note-taking. Isaac responded expressing interest in learning more about how it could help his team scale.

15-minute qualification call with Isaac Sharma. Discussed current state of CS operations at BoldLabs and pain points around manual meeting follow-up. Isaac mentioned they're currently using Fathom but aren't fully satisfied. Identified rep productivity as primary use case with 22 potential users across CS and sales teams.

questions_asked.question: How does your automation compare to Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: Our unlimited automation tier allows custom workflows and CRM sync rules without usage caps, whereas Fathom limits automation features on lower tiers

questions_asked.question: What's the biggest friction point with your current process?
questions_asked.answer: CS reps spend 30-40 minutes per day on manual follow-up tasks that should be automated

next_steps: Isaac to share team structure details via email

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with case study showing 60% reduction in post-meeting admin time for similar mid-market CS teams. Isaac replied with team breakdown (22 seats: 15 CS, 7 sales) and confirmed interest in exploring unlimited automation capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Can you share pricing ranges for 22 seats?
questions_asked.answer: Provided ballpark estimate of $15-20k ACV depending on tier and features selected

next_steps: Schedule discovery call to map workflows

touches.ai_summary: 20-minute discovery call diving into specific workflows. Isaac walked through their current CS handoff process and post-call follow-up requirements. Discussed integration needs with their Salesforce instance and Slack workspace. Isaac confirmed Dylan Roberts (SVP Revenue) would need to approve any tool purchase.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like 90 days after rollout?
questions_asked.answer: CS team hits follow-up SLAs consistently and sales team has better visibility into customer health signals from calls

questions_asked.question: Do you offer multi-language support for customer calls?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we support 40+ languages with automatic detection and transcription

next_steps: Schedule demo for Isaac and 2-3 team members

touches.ai_summary: 40-minute product demo with Isaac and two Account Managers (Savannah Kim joined as technical evaluator). Walked through live call recording, AI summarization, CRM auto-logging, and custom automation workflows. Team was impressed with Salesforce integration depth and automation flexibility beyond what Fathom currently provides.

questions_asked.question: How does the AI handle technical CS troubleshooting calls vs sales calls?
questions_asked.answer: Demonstrated context-aware summarization that adapts to call type and can extract support tickets from troubleshooting discussions

questions_asked.question: Can we customize which fields auto-populate in Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, showed field mapping interface with conditional logic based on call content

questions_asked.question: What's your data retention and security model?
questions_asked.answer: SOC 2 Type II certified, data encrypted at rest and in transit, configurable retention policies from 30 days to indefinite

objections: Savannah noted that team is already paying for Fathom and would need strong ROI case to justify switching
next_steps: Send ROI calculator and competitive comparison doc
Schedule exec meeting with Dylan Roberts

touches.ai_summary: Exec meeting with Dylan Roberts rescheduled from original time. Meeting did not occur.

44-minute exec meeting with Dylan Roberts and Isaac Sharma. Presented business case focused on revenue team efficiency and customer retention impact. Dylan asked pointed questions about implementation timeline, change management, and true cost of switching from Fathom. Discussed unlimited automation tier benefits for scaling their team from 300 to 500 employees over next 18 months.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline and what resources do we need to commit?
questions_asked.answer: 2-week implementation with 5-10 hours total effort from their team, mostly configuration and workflow mapping

questions_asked.question: How do you justify the premium over Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: Walked through unlimited automation ROI - estimated 15 hours/week saved across team, plus better CRM data quality driving forecast accuracy

questions_asked.question: What does support look like during rollout and ongoing?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM for first 90 days, then ongoing support via Slack connect and quarterly business reviews

questions_asked.question: Is there budget allocated this quarter or would this need to wait?
questions_asked.answer: Dylan confirmed budget available but wants to see formal proposal with pricing options

objections: Dylan concerned about disruption from changing tools mid-quarter
next_steps: Send formal proposal with 3 pricing tiers
Include implementation plan and change management guide
Loop in Grace from procurement for contracting

touches.ai_summary: 19-minute negotiation call with Isaac following Dylan's review of proposal. Isaac indicated they're leaning toward unlimited automation tier ($17.7k) but requested pricing flexibility. Discussed contract terms and requested quarterly payment option instead of annual upfront.

questions_asked.question: Can we get a discount if we commit to annual payment?
questions_asked.answer: Offered 5% discount for annual prepay, bringing total to ~$16.8k

questions_asked.question: Is the discount the main blocker or are there other concerns?
questions_asked.answer: Isaac mentioned Dylan wants to derisk the switch from Fathom - quarterly payment feels safer for first year

objections: Quarterly payment strongly preferred over annual prepay
next_steps: Check with finance on quarterly payment terms
Schedule follow-up call end of week

touches.ai_summary: 26-minute final negotiation call with Isaac. Confirmed quarterly payment option approved with 2% discount ($17.35k total). Discussed procurement process with Grace. Isaac committed to getting Dylan's final signature by end of week. Reviewed implementation kickoff timeline for early next month.

questions_asked.question: Can we add 3 additional seats mid-contract if we expand the pilot?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, seats can be added anytime - prorated to contract end date

questions_asked.question: What happens to our Fathom data during migration?
questions_asked.answer: Provided migration guide - historical transcripts can be bulk imported if needed

questions_asked.question: Any other stakeholders who need to weigh in before Dylan signs?
questions_asked.answer: Grace will handle legal review, Isaac and Dylan are the decision makers

next_steps: Send revised proposal with quarterly terms at $17.7k (holding full list price)
